title: doria arbuscula
intro: Doria arbuscula, also known as dwarf juniper or savin juniper, is a low-growing evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and roots have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including their ability to act as a diuretic and to stimulate the kidneys. Doria arbuscula has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and gout, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap the plant's health benefits.
